RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Won on debut at Bath (5f, good) in April and runner-up at Salisbury (5f, good to firm) and Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) on next two starts; hinted last time that today's extra yardage would be a positive; chance.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: He's improved with each of his three runs (all 5f, good), finishing second at Newmarket in April on his second start then first at Pontefract later that month; makes nursery debut after a break and there's every chance that 6f will suit; could have more to offer.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Took a big step forward at Chester (6f, good to soft) on his third start and he showed it was no one-off when again finishing second last time, over this C&D (good to firm); cheekpieces go on; could give another good account.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Left the form of her first three runs well behind to made all in first-time cheekpieces (retained) in a novice at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last Tuesday; could be well treated under a 6lb penalty on her nursery debut.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: Fairies At The Bottom Of The Garden Ltd
Spotlight: He ran his best race yet when fifth of nine at Sandown (5f, good) three weeks ago on his third start, keeping on nicely; he could do with settling better now tackling 6f but otherwise the step up in trip may well prove a positive; firmly in calculations off the back of that latest effort.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise and inexperience when fourth on debut over C&D (good) in May; she hasn't kicked on since and was disappointing at Sandown most recently but it will be no surprise if she bounces back with a big run on her nursery debut; watch the betting.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Upped in trip and switched to front-running tactics at Newcastle (7f, AW debut) last Thursday and he showed improved form in finishing third; he carried his head awkwardly last time and needs to transfer that promise back to turf, but the return to 6f and first-time cheekpieces may both help.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: He's shown ability among his four starts, his best effort a keeping-on fourth of five here (5f, good) in April on his second outing; not ruled out in a first-time visor in his first nursery.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: A Lee, J Matterson, D Mullen & Partner
Spotlight: Has shown a similar level of form in three runs in novice hurdles this year, last time finishing fourth over 2m2f at Kelso (good); each-way chance.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful winner of four 1m2f handicaps on the Flat on soft/good to firm; not as good over hurdles so far, best run when third to two subsequent winners at Sedgefield last time in March (good); cheekpieces tried again (finished lame the previous occasion he wore them); player.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed signs of ability in bumpers but hard to fancy having been pulled up on all three starts over hurdles.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Exposed 14-race maiden over hurdles (placed on seven occasions) who has some fair form to his credit, including when second in a C&D handicap last month (good); likely to find a couple too good.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ex-James Ferguson; fair Flat performer (only win was in a 1m4f Catterick handicap on good to firm); quite a promising hurdles debut when third at Uttoxeter last month (2m, good; 10-1); improvement likely.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed fair form at up to 10.5f in three maiden events on the Flat for Joseph O'Brien; bought for 13,000gns in October; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bought for 7,000euros in May having shown fair form over hurdles for Emmet Mullins, best run when 7.5l fifth of 11 in a Limerick maiden hurdle in March (2m, soft); a possible on stable debut.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Castle View Racing & W F Corbett
Spotlight: Kelso bumper winner last September (2m, good); beaten more than 50l in her three runs over hurdles; perhaps best watched for now.

RaceName: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Pulled up in two mares' events over 2m4f this spring, the latest when tried in a tongue-tie; should improve on first run back after wind op.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 240,000euros yearling; seventh foal; Blue Point sister to 7f AW 2yo winner Archer Royale (RPR 93), half-sister to five winners notably Takeko (Scandinavian 1m/8.6f including Listed) and Hot Team (7f-1m3f including 2yo Listed); one of two newcomers for connections and the betting could be informative.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: J Henwood, E Kelly And A Bright
Spotlight: Improved upon her debut run at Kempton (6f, soft) when runner-up at Goodwood (6f, soft) three weeks ago; she was no match for the winner last time but the third has since gone close; she's one to consider.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Backed into 9-2 when runner-up on recent debut at Ripon (6f, good) behind the heavily odds-on favourite; open to improvement and she's a leading candidate this afternoon.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 180,000gns yearling; second foal; Dark Angel sister to 5f/6f 2yo winner Rum Therapy (RPR 64); dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo/US stakes), half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Isle Of Fernandez; one of two pricey debutantes for connections; interesting to see how she goes in the betting.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 140,000euros breeze-up 2yo; third foal; dam Listed-placed 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; RPR 99) from useful family; likely to improve for this debut run but she could still be a contender, especially if well backed.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 19,000euros foal, £95,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; Persian Force half-sister to French/Czech 7f-1m winner Josephino (including 2yo; RPR 102); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (90), half-sister to Group-placed 5f/6f winner Blockade; with a respected stable and this newcomer could give a good account.

RaceName: Seadell Shops & Holiday Chalets "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: Can lead; course winner who ended a lengthy losing run when making all at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) 12 days ago; a 3lb rise for that success still leaves him well treated on old form.

RaceName: Seadell Shops & Holiday Chalets "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: Won here over 1m1f in April (good to firm) and followed up at Bath (11.5f, firm) next time off a 6lb lower mark than today's; disappointing at Beverley (1m2f) 36 days ago but he did sit close to a strong pace and may be able to bounce back.

RaceName: Seadell Shops & Holiday Chalets "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: Both wins on the Flat have been over longer trips (1m6f/2m); most struggled since winning a maiden over hurdles last June and he was well beaten here over 1m2f in April; off since and others are preferred.

RaceName: Seadell Shops & Holiday Chalets "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: 0-9 but he's finished runner-up on three occasions, most recently when keeping on after being denied a clear run at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) six days ago; now steps up in grade but he's due to be 2lb higher and is a contender returned to this trip.

RaceName: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Solid hurdles strike-rate (4-7); seeks a hat-trick following wins at Huntingdon and Ludlow (both 2m, good; combined rise of 11lb); wouldn't want too much rain - good to soft would be fine - but otherwise holds a clear chance.

RaceName: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: C&D winner who remains 4lb above her last winning mark (Hexham); low key in two C&D starts this year, beaten 19l and 17.5l respectively; this race is easier but she still needs to raise her game.

RaceName: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: A Sinclair, I Jardine & V Renwick
Spotlight: Won over C&D (Class 5, good; same mark) last July; has mixed hurdling with Flat racing this year and wasn't beaten far when third over C&D (good; 7-1, Just Dottie fifth) 25 days ago; highly likely to give his running, even though he isn't a frequent winner.

RaceName: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four C&D wins, the most recent when justifying favouritism from 2lb higher in May 2025; Flat winner since (last September); reportedly never travelling when only fifth of 12 (Ravenscraig Castle third) here on latest start.

RaceName: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seven-race maiden; no real improvement for wind surgery, beaten 13.5l over C&D latest; in good hands but still has plenty of work to do to reverse placings with Ravenscraig Castle (third) and Just Dottie (fifth); best to focus elsewhere.

RaceName: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Naas winner (2m4f maiden hurdle) for Stuart Crawford in December 2024; 0-6 for this trainer, beaten 25l at Hexham (2m, good; 9-1) six weeks ago; mark continues to slide, now 11lb lower than three starts back; others are more obvious.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Won on debut at Bath (5f, good) in April then good third in warm race at Chester (5f, good) in May; well beaten at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last time but gelded since and could go well if returning to form.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: He's run seven times and his sole win came in a seller at Goodwood (5f, soft) in June but that was a valuable race and he's continued to run well, beaten a neck in a novice at Redcar (5f, good) last time; tends to race prominently and he can make another bold bid in a first-time visor.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Went close on debut at Bath (5f, good) then won at Musselburgh (5f, good); not quite at that level when third in Bath novice (5f, firm) in May but she retains potential and could have a part to play.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Improved upon her first two runs when keeping on for close second in a seller at Musselburgh (5f, good), having been outpaced; makes nursery debut off a realistic mark, assuming she can back up that latest effort.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Displayed ability at big prices in May on first two starts (5f, good/good to firm) then well beaten at Royal Ascot; today's nursery debut provides a more realistic opening but others arrive with more pressing claims.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Improved upon her first two runs when third of 11 at Bath (5.7f, good) four weeks ago, beaten just over 4l; she needs another step forward on this first nursery start, but that's possible.

RaceName: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: 4l runner-up at Ascot (5f, good) on second start and similar form when third of five at Ffos Las (5f, soft) last time; the return to better ground could be a plus and her pedigree provides hope that she can be better than her opening mark.

RaceName: Weatherbys EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; colt by Ardad; fourth foal; brother to 6f/7f winner Farough (including 2yo/KSA; RPR 83), half-brother to 6f AW 2yo winner Maui Breeze (76); dam runner-up 5f/6f (87), closely related to 1m2f/10.7f winner Fiji, out of dual 7f Group 3 winner; interesting newcomer and the market should be informative.

RaceName: Weatherbys EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 850,000gns yearling; colt by Night Of Thunder; second foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Chicago Call (RPR 98); dam Group-placed 6f winner (96), half-sister to US winners Fashion Runaway (stakes-placed 6.5f dirt) and A Terrific Shot (6f/7f dirt), out of US 5.5f dirt 2yo winner; lots to like on paper and his powerful connections won this in 2024.

RaceName: Weatherbys EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 5-2 on his debut at Goodwood (5f, good) 13 days ago but faded as if the outing was needed, finishing last of five; steps up in trip with a hood applied and may take a step forward.

RaceName: Weatherbys EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to 5f 2yo winner End Of Story (RPR 90); showed little when last of ten on his debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm; 50-1) last month and a big step forward is needed over this longer trip.

RaceName: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Abbadis Racing & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: Veteran who has won six chases, three over C&D on good/heavy, all in September; has shaped as though needing the race when below par in two runs this year (first was over hurdles); could go well.

RaceName: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ex-pointer; lightly raced maiden under rules; fair run when eighth of 14 in a Kilbeggan beginners' chase in May (2m4f, good) and ran to a similar level of form in a maiden hurdle last time (2m3f, good to yielding); interesting on handicap debut.

RaceName: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has won four chases, all over this trip, the latest at Newcastle in October (good); fair third at Hexham last time (2m4f, good); a possible.

RaceName: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not a frequent winner but both his wins (from 37 jump starts) have been over 2m here; stays 2m4f and, after a fair third here last time (2m), is not without a chance

RaceName: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won two handicap hurdles for Stuart Crawford (2m/2m4f, good/good to firm) but yet to prove he is as effective over fences.

RaceName: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ordinary form over hurdles for Gordon Elliott; bought for £1,800 in May and now goes chasing on his stable debut; bit to prove.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th October Handicap
Spotlight: Down the field on handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, good) in June but bounced back six days later at Lingfield (11.6f, good), where he was runner-up in that novice contest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); he could still have more to offer for his top yard and he's one to consider.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th October Handicap
OwnerName: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster
Spotlight: Improved upon her first three runs for half-length win at 11-2 on recent handicap debut at Ffos Las (1m2f, soft), beating four rivals; cost 290,000gns in 2024 and every chance that she will continue to progress.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th October Handicap
Spotlight: He's raced four times this season and has suffered heavy defeats on three occasions, but two starts ago he easily made all at Catterick (1m4f, good to firm) and it might be dangerous to write him off.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th October Handicap
Spotlight: She could do with settling better back up in trip on handicap debut but she's by Sea The Stars and the way she rallied for third at Ripon (1m, good), having been unbalanced on the undulating track, suggests today's distance could prove a good fit; cost 210,000euros in 2024; interesting.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th October Handicap
Spotlight: On a handy mark on last year's form up to 8.6f and it's possible the drop back in trip will help, but she's been beaten a long way on both starts this season (1m4f/11.4f) and has something to prove.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th October Handicap
Spotlight: Today's handicap debut provides a first realistic opportunity and he's been gelded since last time, with the step back up in trip another possible positive; however, improvement is necessary off his opening mark.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dam 7f-1m3f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 111); strong in the betting and made a winning debut at Newmarket (7f, good; evens) 13 days ago, pulling well clear of the rest with the runner-up who had the benefit of experience; looks a nice prospect for her powerful connections and is the one to beat under a penalty.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dam 7f 2yo winner (including AW/Group 3; RPR 104); played up before beating only one rival home her debut at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) last month and finished last of ten after missing the break here over 6f two weeks ago; steps up in trip with much better needed in a first-time hood.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 85,000gns yearling; filly by St Mark's Basilica; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Royal Scimitar (6f-1m including 2yo/Bahrain; RPR 102), Grigio (1m4f AW; 73) and Bushwacker (Swedish 6f dirt); dam unraced sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Alhebayeb, half-sister to 5f Listed winner Humidor; one to note in the market on her debut.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dubawi filly; sixth foal; closely related to French 6.7f-1m winner Fortuna Diamond (including Listed; RPR 110), half-sister to three winners including Arabian Light (1m-1m2f including AW; 114) and Sapphire Seas (9.4f-1m3f including AW/Listed; 108); dam 7f/1m winner (including AW 2yo/Listed; 103); newcomer with a smart pedigree and looks interesting even though William Buick rides Musical Times.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Filly by Space Blues; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Wetsand (7f/1m 2yo; RPR 86), Perisher (1m; 85) and Gnat Alley (6f including 2yo; 76); dam unplaced 1m 2yo (64), half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Glen Shiel, out of 8.5f Group 3 winner; market may be the best guide on her debut.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-6 pointing in Ireland; has not shown a great deal over hurdles for Olly Murphy, although the form of his first race (only go at 2m4f) has worked out quite well, so worth considering on his handicap debut with tongue-tie now tried.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 1-15 pointing in Ireland but 0-16 under rules; placed nine times, including on latest start when 14l second in a Downpatrick handicap hurdle (2m6f, good to yielding); ran another creditable race when fourth over 3m2f here on previous start (good to soft).

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Comes here in good form having won over 2m here two runs back (good; beat King Kodiak by just over 1l); fair third off this 7lb higher mark at Hexham last time (2m, good); seems to stay 2m4f and should go well.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: C&D winner off 6lb lower at this meeting last season when with Adam Nicol (good); has since won at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft) but has a bit to find with Betteryouthanme on recent Hexham form.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has shown very little at long odds in his three starts, all at around 2m, but hugely unexposed and open to plenty of improvement now up in trip on handicap debut with cheekpieces tried.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won off a 7lb lower mark at Newcastle in March (2m4f, good to soft); fair run when fourth stepped up to 3m at Southwell last time; each-way chance.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran one of his best races since going handicapping when second to Betteryouthanme (who's now 4lb worse off for just over 1l) over 2m at Hexham last time (good; 33-1); has run creditably at this trip; a possible.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: Tough veteran who has won 11 races, four of them here (2m/2m4f); has been running well recently, second over 2m at Hexham last time (good); player.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7; well beaten on his last three starts, all here, the latest on his handicap debut when tried over 3m; plenty to prove.

RaceName: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed twice over 2m7f at Hexham in May last year; well below form after a break on next two starts, all for Maurice Barnes; has had another break since and has come a long way to make his stable debut; hood now tried; a possible.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up twice in a row (7f, good to firm) before winning at Wetherby (good to firm) recently on her first crack at 1m; she could have more to offer at about this trip and she's respected up 4lb.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Second on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in May and subsequent fifth at Newbury (1m, good to soft) came in a race that suited those ridden more patiently; has shown promise on good going; not ruled out.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: 2-3 in 2yo campaign (6f, AW/good) but a long way below that level on her four starts this year (5f-7f); the step up in trip needs to prompt a dramatic turnaround.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Reappeared over C&D (good) in May having been gelded and finished third in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on that handicap debut, despite being slowly away; he could be sharper for that outing and he's on the shortlist.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: She's dropping down the weights but has failed to finish better than sixth since winning on her debut at Southwell (6f, AW) last November and needs to raise her game over this new trip.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: This is her first run since last November but her trainer had a productive June and she showed promise on the middle of her three 2yo starts, when a close sixth of nine at Leicester (1m, good to soft) in October; the step up in trip and first-time hood may be positives on handicap debut.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row (1m, soft/good) prior to a clear, close second behind a well-treated rival at Musselburgh (1m, good) last Monday; she returns to action before a 5lb rise kicks in and she has leading claims.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Went close at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March and respectable fourth at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in April, but two lesser runs have followed and he needs to leave those performances well behind.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: No wins from 12 starts on the Flat, leaving him with a rating of 48; well beaten in a Ballinrobe claimer last month; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Narrowly won at Dundalk on Flat handicap debut in a first-time tongue tie (7f); below that level at the Curragh next time and gelded since; of obvious interest now hurdling given his Flat rating of 75 and connections; hood goes on.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Big price at Thurles in a 1m maiden and ran accordingly; gave trouble at the start and withdrawn in two intended starts since then; goes hurdling now but others preferred.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed twice in Roscommon maidens at Roscommon last year and again twice on AW earlier this year; claimed for 5,000euros after disappointing in Ballinrobe claimer in May; not too bad in a Down Royal maiden last time; rated 63 on the Flat and not discounted.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten a length and then a head in a pair of 1m maidens at Dundalk in November; too keen in early stages on handicap debut at Navan over 1m2f in May; well beaten in a maiden back at Dundalk last time; holds the top Flat rating (80) in this field and is courtesy of those efforts; big player.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Good start to handicapping on the Flat when runner-up over 1m at this course (yielding); okay fourth at Leopardstown next time (1m, soft to heavy); brings a Flat rating of 65 to this first attempt over hurdles and he could play a hand.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten 42l on debut in a Fairyhouse Flat maiden (1m4f, good); hard to fancy after that.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Rated 44 on the Flat; modest form for Jack Davison and beaten 27l on stable debut at Gowran last time; unlikely.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Australia gelding; purchased for 27,000euros as a foal and improved in value to 85,000euros as a yearling; dam French 7.5f AW winner, half-sister to Italian 1m2f Group 3 winner American Bridge, out of French 7f 2yo Listed winner; interesting profile; check market.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Holds a Flat rating of 48 after being well beaten in Flat maidens and on handicap debut; tries hurdling now but others make more appeal.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came in novice at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in November and she ran well on her handicap debut at Newcastle (AW) next time; strong in the market on her stable debut at Newmarket (7f, good; first-time hood) 13 days ago but proved disappointing; steps back up in trip and it will be interesting to see if her supporters return.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner over 1m and he was runner-up on turf over this C&D two weeks ago on his first start for 270 days; the winner has won again since; has major claims in the returning cheekpieces.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins on soft/heavy ground over 7f in 2024; quite inconsistent since but did run respectably when fourth of 12 at Brighton (1m, good) 23 days ago; cheekpieces back on.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: 11-race maiden but his best two efforts have been over this C&D; probably didn't stay 1m2f here 35 days ago and has since been dropped a further 3lb; tongue-tie now goes on and he has possibilities back at 1m.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort when fourth in a maiden at Redcar (7f, good to firm) last August; well held in three handicaps since, latest on his reappearance at Lingfield (7f, good; first start since gelding operation) 31 days ago; hood added.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Profited from a final-fence mistake from Defying Gravity to make winning C&D chase debut (good to soft; 7-4 favourite, from 6lb lower) in May; didn't jump with fluency when runner-up at Worcester (beaten over 8l) next time; back from wind surgery, with headgear also tweaked.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
OwnerName: County Set Four & L V Russell
Spotlight: Maiden (0-19); runner-up at Kelso (3m, good) last October but out of form since; in rear throughout over 2m4f at Hexham (beaten 27l) latest; it isn't easy to build a compelling case for his chance.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Continued his progress, turning over an odds-on stablemate of The Flying Poet at Market Rasen (2m3f, good; from 6lb lower; fences in the home straight were omitted) three weeks ago; his win in a small field there the time before was over nearly 2m7f; looks hard to beat in hat-trick mission, if proving equally effective over 3m.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 1-25 strike-rate; runner-up to Burgundy Man over C&D (soft) last October; underwhelming in two starts this year, beaten 47l at Downpatrick (3m, good) 19 days ago; blinkers are now added.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Kept on to account for Thehairyfella over C&D (soft) last October; fortunes have dipped markedly since and he weakened quickly when beaten 45l over C&D (hurdle) latest; wind surgery and an added tongue-tie could prompt a revival from what is a workable mark; consult the market.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-8; scuppered his chance by making a final-fence mistake over C&D (The Flying Poet took full advantage) in May; unseated rider early over fences at Kelso next time and far from fluent back over hurdles at Cartmel latest; risks attached.

RaceName: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Best hurdles form (two wins) on soft or heavy; placed in two of his three starts over fences, albeit weakening at Uttoxeter (3m; beaten over 13l) 19 days ago; reportedly bled from the nose at Fontwell on penultimate start; task isn't impossible.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Slow starts can be an issue and he couldn't get into it when sixth at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) recently, but it wasn't a bad run and before that he was second at Newbury (1m, good); the step back up in trip may help and he might not be far away; the visor (worn on last two runs) is left off.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Hard to fancy on the evidence of the last 12 months but he was useful in his prime and he's one to watch in the betting off a reduced mark on his third start for this yard.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on stable debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February but unable to get competitive since and needs something extra today.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: 0-12 on turf but ran well when fourth of 14 at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) in April and didn't enjoy a clear run when fifth of eight there (1m2f, good to soft) last time; has an each-way shout.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Showed little on first three starts but made handicap/stable debut at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) recently having been gelded and showed promise in fifth; that was his first outing since January and he shaped as though he may improve for the run; firmly in calculations.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Bath (1m, firm) in April on second handicap start and followed up at Redcar (1m, good) before 2l second of three back at Bath (1m, firm); there can be optimism that his latest defeat doesn't represent the limit of his ability and this step up in trip is worth exploring; key player.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Kept on for encouraging second at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) in May on her third start; down the field in both handicaps (1m, good) but it's still very early days and perhaps first-time cheekpieces will help.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: 0-7 and his claims aren't compelling, but soft ground may not have suited this 3yo last time and there have been signs of promise previously; edging down the weights and things may click at some point.

RaceName: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who has been runner-up in two of her three handicaps, most recently at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last Tuesday when beaten a short head; runs off the same mark today and she's a leading contender.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: French import; won a 2m2f hurdle race on good ground in May last year; an interesting recruit to a leading local yard; has a big burden, but Calum Hogan takes off a useful 5lb; worth a market check.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Last of three Flat wins for Jessica Harrington was off 84 last summer; was having her eighth hurdles start when making the breakthrough in a Wexford handicap in May; failed to back that up at Listowel.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Downpatrick maiden hurdle winner in May 2025; fair form in novice hurdles since; travelled well before finding little when sixth on handicap debut at Down Royal; winning chase debut at Clonmel last time; merits respect, worth checking his market position relative to the stable's Eastmore.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Not a bad effort at Cork in May after a long absence; confidence dented by Listowel run last month, looked a difficult ride, pulling hard at times and hanging.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner for Liam O'Brien (good to yielding) in 2024; mixed signals from his form for Gordon Elliott; never in the hunt at Kilbeggan last time after an early mistake; better judged on previous Bellewstown second.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Some good placed efforts in maidens on good ground; placed three times between August and October last year; modest winter form; better ground should suit and the addition of cheekpieces may help.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed ability in bumpers; began over hurdles with a low-key Wexford run; much improved when fifth at Clonmel; won well at Kilbeggan despite racing keenly; opening mark gives him a fair chance.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Held his form well after winning a Down Royal handicap last August (2m1f, good to firm); back from a break, he appeared to get outstayed by the front two over 2m3f at Cork; no match for an easy winner at Tramore; one of the main contenders here.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed some ability in three maidens; solid effort on handicap/seasonal debut at Wexford, finished third despite showing a tendency to hang left; young mare, can improve.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Exors of the Late M Mortell
Spotlight: Fairly consistent before winning a 2m handicap hurdle at the Listowel festival, an event well targeted by her trainer; probably needed Ballinrobe run in May.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: AW winner for Karl Burke early last year; regressive in turf handicaps; hinted at handicap potential at Naas in March on his third maiden hurdle start, beaten 28l in sixth behind a Willie Mullins-trained odds-on chance in a 20-runner contest.

RaceName: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In good form before a winter break; useful sort, but has a definite preference for longer trips; four-time winner in points, most recent of five tracks wins was gained over 2m7f.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; placed in a maiden at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last June but he's been well held in seven starts since; failed to beat a rival over 1m2f last time and blinkers are now tried.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner for Roger Varian (7f/1m; AW/good to firm) and he's now 13lb lower than for the second win; ran his best race since joining this stable when sixth of 18 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft; first-time visor) 18 days ago and has claims if building on that; headgear removed.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: Seven wins (5f/6f), the most recent at Lingfield (6f, good) last May off a 13lb higher mark than today's; below his best 13 days ago and his stamina is a concern on his first attempt over 1m.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at Brighton (6f, good; first-time hood) last August and had the hood removed when coming home nicely to win at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in January; off for 122 days before finishing a creditable third over this C&D in May and has solid claims on his first start since.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win when getting up late on over 1m at Kempton (AW) in December off 6lb higher than today; hasn't gone on from that and he's been comfortably held in his three starts on turf for this stable; others preferred.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: 17-race maiden who has beaten only one rival home in two starts on the AW since joining this stable from Alice Haynes; others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Mark Sumner Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was twice third on the AW last year but has been well held in five starts on turf, including here over 1m2f in April when last seen; drop back in trip needs to make a difference.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Won novice at Newcastle (7f, AW) in April on belated debut and better form when close fourth in C&D novice five weeks ago, both under 7lb claimer; plenty in this field have a form chance and he is one of them.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 110,000gns yearling by New Bay; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Harold Shand (7f 2yo; RPR 85) and Unpresuming (Hong Kong 7f; 71); dam unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Toormore and 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Estidhkaar; hooded newcomer from a leading yard and the market should guide.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Plenty of ability when taking minor honours at this track in a 7f novice in March and 1m maiden (close from the front) in April; in the mix.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Two runs on turf, finishing a lot closer at Brighton (6f, good to firm; made most) last time but he was only fifth of six and it was modest form; the equipment he wore that day (hood and cheekpieces) is dispensed with.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: $100,000 yearling, 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo; refused to enter stalls on intended debut in May; 28-1, slowly away and always behind at Windsor (1m, good to soft) just over three weeks ago; pedigree gives hope for AW surface.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Close third over C&D last August, which requires some respect even though it came in between two heavy defeats (back here latest); gelded since; market can guide for his first run in 300 days.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 10,000gns foal by Sergei Prokofiev; first foal; dam 7f-8.6f winner (including AW; RPR 78), half-sister to fairly useful winners Fiscal Policy (5f/6f) and Dittander (5f 2yo), out of German 1,000 Guineas winner; probably up against it on debut.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Not beaten far on debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last November and reappeared with good second in slowly run race there (8.6f, AW) one month ago; refused to enter stalls at Salisbury (7f) three weeks ago; if there's no repeat of that, she's one of the several leading form contenders.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Failed to justify market prominence at Newbury (6f, good) last August on sole run as 2yo; a bit better there (7f, good) on May return but weakened into eighth of 15 and others in this line-up have accomplished more.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 5-4 favourite when only fifth of six on debut at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) four weeks ago; always-prominent second of 11 at Southwell (6f, AW) two weeks later, however, and that makes her a contender.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won chase debut at Sedgefield (2m5f, good; from 2lb lower) in April but has posted form figures of PUP since; it's difficult to know what to expect, but he does revert to hurdles at an appropriate level and has run well here (second and third) in the past.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Continues to slide in the weights, now 10lb below his last winning mark (2m4f, Hexham); Kelso fourth (2m5f; in first-time blinkers) was okay but his latest Hexham effort (2m) was a poor one; plenty to prove, particularly over 3m.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Narrow Down Royal winner (3m, good to yielding) last October, prior to career-best third at Ascot (3m;) the following month; less competitive in 2026, beaten 26l over C&D latest; cheekpieces fitted with renewed spark needed.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Pounced late at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in February 2025; steadily working back after a lengthy absence, with his Uttoxeter third (2m4f) last time a creditable effort; stamina reserves untested over 3m.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: Perth regular, with three course wins, including from 3lb higher in this last year; plugged on from an impossible position to finish a modest third over C&D (Creadan Grace fifth) 25 days ago; he's into the twilight of his career, so others make more appeal.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Defied the market drift to make a winning handicap debut over 2m4f here (good to soft; in added cheekpieces) in May; despite being a beaten 11-10 favourite, she ran just as well when third over 3m (off this mark) at Uttoxeter latest; leading contender.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up in a point; six-race rules maiden, soundly beaten in novice and maiden company (2m-2m4f) and faring no better - forward move petered out - on recent handicap debut at Hexham; tackles a new trip (rules) with Derek Fox back aboard.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had tumbled down the weights, with no win since September 2023, until springing a 66-1 surprise over 2m4f here (good to soft) in May; retains handicapping scope from this mark (5lb higher) but it remains to be seen if he's in the same fettle over 3m; chance.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 15-race maiden; beaten when nearly unshipping her rider two out over C&D on penultimate start; did too much in front, weakening from the last, at Hexham (3m, good; fifth of 11) subsequently; Brian Hughes is booked so, if ridden with more restraint, she isn't discounted.

RaceName: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7; pulled up in three of his last four, including at odds of 300-1 at Musselburgh (2m, soft) last December; hood added, but he surely isn't the solution off a 206-day break from 9lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal cost 37,000euros; looked in need of the experience when fifth of eight on 7f Leopardstown debut three weeks ago, weakening late on; behind Cashel Queen and Celtic Moon on that occasion so needs to improve.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Paul J Nolan/Walerc Limited/M J Wasylocha/Tony Cantwell
Spotlight: Sixth foal cost 20,000euros as yearling; half-sister to winners Lady Of Inishfree (7f 2yo; RPR 94) and Chukotka (KSA 1m2f); dam 1m2f turf/1m4f AW winner (83), out of 12.5f Group 2 winner; stable 0-4 with 2yos this year; hooded.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cheap purchase was well beaten in Naas barrier trial but better than bare result on 6f debut at Fairyhouse, weakening late on after forceful tactics early; needs to find more but hood may help.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam placed 6f 2yo, half-sister to winners Oh Purple Reign (Listed-placed 6f-1m) and Warsaw Road (7f); dam placed 6f 2yo (70); stable 0-39 with 2yos; best watched.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Behind some of these on debut at Leopardstown and poor effort at Down Royal; work to do.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sixth foal; 35,000gns 2yo; half-sister to Italian 7.5f/1m winner; dam 7f AW winner, out of half-sister to winners Tipsy Creek (5f Group 2), Abunawwas (7f Group 3) and Magic Cove (1m Listed); stable won three of last six runnings of this; shortlisted.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Slowly away on 6.5f debut at Listowel but it was a respectable fourth behind the winner Beibhinn who finished third in a Group 2 subsequently, while runner-up Big Negotiator won at York before midfield in the Queen Mary; could improve up in trip.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Stuck to her task well when landing Naas barrier trial in May; solid debut effort at Leopardstown (7f, soft) when beaten 5l, finishing ahead of Celtic Moon, Cloughleagh Bridge and Caoch The Piper; sets the standard and the promise of better ground should suit.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Thunder Moon filly caught the eye when beaten 0.25l behind Cashel Queen in fourth on debut at Leopardstown three weeks ago; stable among the winners and improvement is likely; in the mix.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
OwnerName: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Two wins this season (6f/5f, good/good to soft), including here, and when fourth of five here (6f, good) recently he wasn't beaten far following an awkward start; major player if the stalls break isn't an issue this time.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
OwnerName: D Morgan And K J Parris
Spotlight: Five AW wins since last September (6f/5f) and beaten only about 4l when ninth of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) two months ago, when last seen; this is less competitive and she could be involved.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: Multiple winner, the latest last July when 10lb higher; a market check is advised but this 8yo has been well beaten on his last three starts and needs to turn things around.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
OwnerName: R M Brilley, A E Townsend & Partner
Spotlight: Made all at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in February and returned from a break with good third at Thirsk (5f, good) recently, when making the running under Hope Regan; could make another bold bid.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: Won over C&D (good) in April on reappearance before close third at Beverley (5f, good to firm); unplaced on next two starts but she was slowly away when sixth at Musselburgh then drawn widest when fifth at Beverley (5f, good); she's one to consider.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: Ended his last campaign in good form and he reappeared with a respectable fourth at Beverley (5f, good) 16 days ago; he could show the benefit of that latest outing today and he's run some good races over this C&D.

RaceName: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)
Spotlight: C&D winner who returned to form when third at Ripon (5f, good) in May and is 5lb lower today; however, this 7yo has struggled on both starts since and has something to prove.

RaceName: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)
Spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner; now 2-10 over fences having made all to score readily over 2m4f Listowel a month ago; won this race last year as a prelude to a fine effort in taking second place in the Galway Plate; faces a tough task this time, since he meets Daddy Long Legs on 12lb worse than handicap terms.

RaceName: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)
Spotlight: Admirably consistent handicapper who brought his tally to five with two wins at Leopardstown last winter; ran in the County Hurdle before an ambitious Grade 2 attempt at Fairyhouse; has won on good ground, would probably prefer an ease.

RaceName: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)
Spotlight: Course Grade 3 winner in 2024, currently rated 150 after Ballinrobe win in May; previously may have needed the run when well beaten in a Southwell race won by Constitution Hill; beaten by the unconsidered Counting Coup in a Flat maiden at Down Royal 12 days ago; stylish Ballinrobe success in first-time cheekpieces makes him the one to beat at these weights.

RaceName: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)
Spotlight: Useful chasing campaign last season returned three wins from seven starts; not at his best in a Newbury Grade 2 last November or in Aintree Grade 1 in April; 140 hurdles rating puts him 8lb behind Bowensfire. might be more effective than his stablemate on this ground.

RaceName: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)
Spotlight: Smart dual-purpose performer, although he has been lightly raced since taking a 3m1f Kilbeggan conditions hurdle in August 2024; absent since last October; would receive 16lb from Daddy Long Legs in a handicap.

RaceName: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)
Spotlight: Progressive in handicaps in the first half of last season; gained a third handicap success in 2m4f Clonmel mares' event in October; was found wanting in conditions races later; tough task in this company; cheekpieces on for the first time.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins have been on the AW (7f/1m), the most recent at Chelmsford last August, but he returned to form at Windsor (6f, good to firm) ten days ago, finishing well to be second of 11; won't mind the step back up in trip and he's shortlisted.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins on turf have been at Doncaster (7f) on good to firm; close third of 11 at Newcastle (7f, AW) in December but ran below his best when last seen 147 days ago; break may have done him some good.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who is thriving at present and won three of his last four starts, two of them over 7f at Brighton and the other at Bath over 5.5f; ran another solid race at the former track on Tuesday and can go well again.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins have been over 7f at Southwell (Karl Burke/Iain Jardine); has dropped a long way in the weights and took second on his stable debut at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago; steps back up in trip with solid claims.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner over 6f, most recently at Kempton in May off a 1lb lower mark than today's; third on turf at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) after that success but he's been below that level over this trip in two starts since.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW in 2025 when previously trained by this yard and returned to some form on his first start since rejoining this stable at Southwell (7f, AW) in May, running on well for second; left the impression he's still in that form at Lingfield 28 days ago but turf is a slight question mark.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when winning on soft ground over this C&D three weeks ago (third start since being gelded); back up 3lb and likely has quicker ground to handle today.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden; posted a solid effort when fourth of 13 on his stable debut at Kempton (1m, AW; first start since being gelded) in May but he was tailed off at Doncaster (7f, good) 18 days ago; bit to prove on grass.

RaceName: Branfords Handicap
Spotlight: Stepped forward from her debut when fourth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May but she didn't beat a rival at Wolverhampton (6f, AW; beaten 19l) one month ago; makes her handicap debut and may do better.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 220,000gns yearling; late foal; brother to two useful 7f winners, but this 3yo was beaten 18l over C&D (33-1) in April and just over 10l at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; 22-1) in early June; modest form.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Half-brother to winners Sajir (5.5f-7f including 2yo/AW/Group 1; RPR 121) and First Kingdom (1m including AW; 92); about 13l sixth of ten in C&D novice (12-1) five weeks ago was modest form on debut, fading, but he could be better with that under his belt.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Third twice over C&D last autumn and same again when close up at Southwell (6f, AW) on reappearance; best form was on second start (before wind surgery) and he kept on stoutly last time, suggesting a return to 7f would suit; gelded since; firmly in calculations.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 450,000gns yearling; New Bay half-brother to five winners including Romantic Warrior (6f-1m2f including multiple Group/Grade 1s); slowly away when always behind in novice at Salisbury (7f, soft) three weeks ago but he was the 5-2 favourite, so he's presumably thought capable of much better; tongue tied this time and it will be interesting to see if his backers return.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 9-1, finished 9l behind the pretty useful winner in maiden at Lingfield (7f, AW) in November but beat the other eight; gelded this May; could be a marked improver.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Speedy pedigree but no chance in novice at Yarmouth (6f, soft; 16-1) three weeks ago after badly blowing the start; hooded this time.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Triple-figure odds when showing low-level form at Lingfield in September (7f, AW) and two weeks ago (6f, good); hooded in the latter, having refused to enter stalls at Nottingham (300-1) in April; no claims yet.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Not far away on last three starts, particularly when second at Southwell (7f, AW; set pace) and Catterick (6f, good to soft; beaten a neck) three weeks ago; one of the form picks and another bold show should be on its way.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 125-1 and hooded, slow-starting last of eight in maiden at Newmarket (1m, good) in April when trained by Des Donovan.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Penultimate Roscommon run his best yet, good fourth after not clear run in first-time tongue-tie; wide draw and testing ground valid excuses for poor effort at Leopardstown; better run expected here.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Denise McCourt & Stephen Sweeney & Maire Bailey
Spotlight: Out the back in three maidens over trips ranging from 7f-1m4f; handicaps have likely always been the plan so worth a market check for local trainer.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
OwnerName: E D Delany & Mrs Lisa Delany
Spotlight: Down the field in turf/AW contests last year; long-priced C&D winner (soft) in April on first attempt at the trip before solid third at Gowran (7f, soft) from 9lb higher; would probably want rain but holds every chance.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Won 7f Dundalk nursery on AW debut last November when trained by Donnacha O'Brien; didn't quite see out the stiff mile when close fourth at Dundalk on yard debut; completely tailed off over 6f at the Curragh on latest; headgear combination is back on eased in grade.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
OwnerName: Dan Kiely & C O'Neill & Apollo Thoroughbreds
Spotlight: Well held first two handicap starts but not beaten far at Naas in April and solid third of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) on latest; the winner Meriden has gone on to win three more on the bounce (now rated 97) and three further winners have come out of the race; shortlisted up in trip.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: First five runs this year on the AW, best effort on 7f handicap debut when beaten around 5l into fifth; finished last next time and no better on turf debut at Fairyhouse in May; new headgear combination; others preferred.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in three 7f maidens; failed to land a blow on 6f Fairyhouse handicap debut last month; Colin Keane booked stepping up in trip; watch the market for clues.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Triple-figure prices for all three maiden runs when well beaten; cheekpieces on for handicap debut at a more realistic level now handicapping; market will reveal expectations.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens; 66-1 for handicap debut when sixth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good), not far behind Fontaine Khaled and Beau Army; failed to build on that at that venue on soft 13 days ago but could bounce back if the ground is good.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Third placing in a Ballinrobe claimer was a step in the right direction; not without her backers in first two handicap outings but has failed to deliver; tongue-tie is now reached for.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
OwnerName: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & D Battersby
Spotlight: Second runner for local yard; maiden went closest when chasing home a progressive type over 6f at Dundalk in April; has a tendency to miss the break; short of room and hampered 2f out when behind third-placed Fontaine Khaled at Down Royal on latest; not discounted.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Has won over 6f/7f at Dundalk; ran her best race on turf when fourth behind the progressive Meriden at Down Royal on penultimate (one place behind Fontaine Khaled); nowhere near that level at Limerick since but player if at her best.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; never better than fourth in 12 outings; not a bad effort when fifth of 14 at Ballinrobe (1m2f) ten days ago but will need to pull out more to win this.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Weak form in maidens; given a positive ride but ultimately faded out of things on Gowran handicap debut (9.5f); cheekpieces; others look more likely.

RaceName: Champion Lettings Handicap
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; weak turf form last season; maiden's best form is on the AW; has plenty to find judged on two latest efforts at Gowran and Ballinrobe; new headgear combination.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Tenth foal; half-brother to winners Queen Of Cool, Hintsnallegations, All That Remains and Tartan Cookie; dam 1m Flat/hurdle winner.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Flat handicap at Navan in 2024; soundly beaten in two outings over hurdles; not a likely maiden winner at this stage.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Remote ninth on debut at Ballinrobe; Jack Owen Jack is the stable's main hope here.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three stats; his trainer has a leading candidate in Jack Owen Jack.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Won his sole point-to-point in May 2024; shown ability in making the frame in two outings at this track last season; however, a reported history of veterinary issues is a concern.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Similar profile to yard's Lord Lackendarra, well exposed in bumpers; fair hurdle run at Tramore in April; failed to build on that at Killarney the following month.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Solid efforts in bumpers; runner-up at Cork last October (2m3f, good) and when close third at Kilbeggan in May (good);  can feature if jumping better than at Listowel, when he was 4l third, around 2l behind the runner-up, a stablemate of Latopix.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed in three of his 12 starts in bumpers, moderate third in a maiden hurdle at Tramore on his penultimate start.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Fair pedigree; failed to complete two starts in points; Jack Doyle fits him with a tongue-tie for racecourse debut

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Ordinary form in points after finishing second in a four-horse race on his debut; may be able to build on a fair effort on hurdles debut at Clonmel; will probably need to get a handicap mark.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Weak bumper form in 2024; no better at Listowel last month after a long absence.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Getaway 6yo whose dam was a 2m4f hurdle winner, closely related to high-class staying chaser Conflated; has shown some ability in bumpers; chased home an easy winner at Gowran in March; soundly beaten in fifth at Wexford on second attempt.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Second foal; Mahler gelding; dam was placed over hurdles; pedigree credentials are weak.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Fair pedigree; only 5-1 for debut at Clonmel, carried out at the second but continued to finish in rear; looked like a staying type when a rallying fourth in a modest 2m6f maiden at Downpatrick

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: By Maxios out of the brilliant Solerina; remote fourth over C&D on debut, can improve

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Won over 1m4f in France; modest form in two bumper starts in Britain; failed to build on promising hurdling debut third at Gowran (2m, heavy) when tailed off at Navan in December (2m, soft to heavy); leading chance now based on last month's claimer-ridden Punchestown second.

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Sea Moon mare out of a Flat and hurdles winner; well beaten on only point-to-point start

RaceName: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Huge price when showing mild promise with a recent fourth placing at Downpatrick; plenty more improvement needed.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; hasn't lived up to market expectations in two turf runs but her latest third of six at Doncaster (6.5f, good to firm) was a step forward from her debut effort; should continue to improve with time and distance and she's in the mix on nursery debut; stablemate of Indian Land.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Ran green on Bath debut in May but he's shown clear promise with his placed efforts at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and Leicester (7f, good) in last two runs; gave it a good shot from the front when runner-up last time and he's an interesting contender on nursery debut.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Beat only one rival on his Leicester debut but he left that form a long way behind when scoring at Wolverhampton (6f, AW); that form is hard to weigh up but he finished well for a last-gasp success and is open to more progress on this step up to 7f; needs a close look in his first nursery.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Civ & B R Millman
Spotlight: Won a Beverley novice (5f, good to soft) on her second start but she's finished down the field in both subsequent runs; had tough task in the Chesham at Royal Ascot but she's untried on AW and this looks competitive on her nursery debut; others preferred.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Encouraging fourth on debut here (6f) in May but he's taken two backward steps since and was tailed off at Leicester (7f, good) last month; still early days but he weakened quickly last time and has something to prove on nursery debut.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: No progress in maiden/novice events but he did show promise with his debut fourth here (6f) in April; half-brother to a 2yo winner over this trip and he needs watching in market on nursery debut.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner
Spotlight: Oscar Performance filly; ran green on her debut at Wolverhampton (6f) in May but she's finished in rear again in both subsequent runs; now steps up to 7f on nursery debut but she needs plenty of improvement.

RaceName: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Struggled in her three qualifying runs (5f), with a best RPR of 34; this new trip should be a better fit on pedigree but she's 9lb out of the weights on her nursery debut; passed over.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins at up to 1m but 0-19 for this yard; good effort despite racing keenly when close fourth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) on penultimate; dwelt over 6f at Fairyhouse on latest; worth a market check for local handler with Colin Keane booked.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Winner over 1m1f on quick ground in Britain; built on Limerick second last month when scoring decisively at that venue (1m, soft) nine days later; up 6lb but no reason why he can't go well again.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
OwnerName: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan
Spotlight: Sole run at this track resulted in C&D win (good) in April 2025 off 9lb higher; posted a couple of good efforts on the AW early this year; well held next two starts at Leopardstown; step in the right direction there last time when sixth of 14 after a slow break.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner is 0-27 on turf; middling effort at the Curragh last time and overall record on this surface is concerning.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner, including over C&D (good to yielding) last August off 3lb lower; best effort in three runs this year when close third at Limerick (7f, yielding) last month; slow break costly at that track a day later; could bounce back.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Second runner for local yard; best run was at an early stage of last season; a long way below that standard this year; leap of faith required.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Last turf win came at Naas in August 2024 (7f, good); signs he could capitalise on sliding mark when close third at Killarney (1m, good) in May but disappointing favourite at Listowel next time; failed to see out 1m1f on soft at Leopardstown on latest but definite player at his best.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Shaped quite well at Galway last August on third maiden start; not a great deal of promise in handicaps, no better than seventh in four attempts; returns from 239-day absence.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Weak form for Josh Halley; best run for Andy Slattery on yard debut when third in 7f claimer at Dundalk; regressive since; first run for Eoin Griffin; best watched.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney
Spotlight: Third runner for local yard; four-time winner; last turf win in 2023 over 8.5f at Galway (soft); two AW wins since; hard to fancy on this year's form but has placed multiple times over C&D, including when runner-up at this festival last year from a stone higher; can't dismiss.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; six-time winner in Britain; only glimpses of peak form since second in a claimer early last season, including third over C&D off 9lb higher; seventh of 16 over 1m at Gowran on seasonal debut; place chance.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: No wins in 11 but only his second run in this headgear combination when bang there at Cork (1m, yielding) last September; something amiss next time under Colin Keane at Dundalk; has been off the track since.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-time AW winner in Britain for Mick Appleby; no success since 2024; has shown nothing in 15 outings on the Flat and over hurdles for current yard.

RaceName: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap
Spotlight: Fourth runner for local yard; still a maiden after 18 starts, placed four times; had a good spell on AW early in the year; market can separate stable's runners.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time winner who is 3lb beneath his last winning mark; however, he hasn't offered all that much in three runs following a break and wind surgery.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: Won three times at 1m2f (good/good to firm) last year; 0-6 since moving yards and below her best in last four races, the last two in blinkers that are now removed; on a useful mark but a revival is needed.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: A winner on turf but has a superior strike-rate on the AW including a hat-trick earlier this year; only four ran when third at Yarmouth last time and that was under a 5lb claimer; could be vulnerable.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs C Fruhbeck and Denford Stud
Spotlight: Lightly raced filly; runner-up at Kempton (1m) in February before making all in a 7f novice back there two weeks later; bred to stay this longer trip well and her best days could still be ahead of her.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway
Spotlight: Hasn't had a lot of racing and probably his best effort yet in handicaps when a 4l third at Goodwood a month ago (1m1f, good to soft); he still doesn't look on that generous a mark.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: Second to a subsequent Listed winner in August (1m, good to firm) but disappointed in October after a break and hasn't got going this season, including when hooded for handicap debut at Sandown a month ago; downgraded here but with a bit to prove now.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: 0-5; both his handicaps have been at Chester (1m2f; good then good to soft); the third was encouraging and then reportedly unsuited by ground that connections described as tacky; cheekpieces go on this time and hard to discount.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: Had two races for David Menuisier and three for current trainer; nearly won over C&D in May despite not getting the best of runs and soft ground was reportedly unsuitable next time; should run well.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: Well-bred filly who has improved with each run, last time finishing nicely clear of the rest when beaten a length on handicap debut (1m2f, good; 9-2); raised 3lb but that's not the end of the world.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
Spotlight: 65,000gns breeze-up buy who shaped with promise last season in 7f/1m races won by smart horses; absent since October but potentially interesting on this handicap debut now raised in distance; the market could be enlightening.

RaceName: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap
OwnerName: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn
Spotlight: Bang there on his handicap debut at Doncaster in April (1m2f, good to firm) but two lesser runs have followed under similar conditions; capable but couldn't be confident about him.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had been mixing Flat and hurdling action to useful effect before an off-day at Ballinrobe in May; last year's maiden hurdle win was on good ground.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Treaty Retail Invest' Holdings Ltd/Denis Murphy B'stock Ltd
Spotlight: Point winner; runner up in maiden hurdles at Kilbeggan, Killarney and Sligo last summer; beaten almost 10l in third over C&D on handicap debut five weeks ago; should improve from the run, his first since last autumn.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Moderate overall form since Kilbeggan win two years ago; has changed stable since finishing down the field in a Kilbeggan race in which Bhean Saibhre finished fifth; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won as a 2yo in Britain; failed to make much impression over hurdles last winner; different proposition now; has gone up 13lb for his improved performances (second, followed by a win) at Clonmel this season; sustained progress is feasible

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Four Fools & A Fast Horse Syndicate
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Britain; promoted winner of C&D handicap in which Friar Hogan was carried out; he had also suffered interference caused by the first-past-the-post; went up 6lb and failed to distinguish himself at Punchestown.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Thurles bumper winner on debut last October; set a couple of stiff tasks over hurdles in recent months; sights lowered now for handicap debut.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has been chasing, pulled up on latest; 1-33 hurdles record is a significant negative but he has a decent record in place terms; course winner over fences two seasons ago.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a 2m2f beginners' chase at Downpatrick (yielding) last autumn; placed over hurdles on either side of that first success; might need this on first outing after a lengthy break.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner; endured a dismal spell of form in the first half of the year, when pulled up in three consecutive chase starts; signs of a recovery over hurdles recently.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; 1-17 over hurdles; entitled to need this after missing last season.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner, ordinary form in maiden hurdles; should be more effective in handicaps; worth a market check.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Very solid bumper form, taking the runner-up spot three times between June and August two years ago; placed in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle last year; more recent hurdles form is much less convincing; may find her level in handicaps; good 5lb claimer booked.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner; some promise in maiden hurdles at Punchestown and Thurles in 2024; has struggled in two handicap hurdles after a spell over fences; stablemate Warm In Gorey is more appealing.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Not far off the pace when carried out at the second-last over C&D on latest, a race awarded to today's rival Qaasid on the disqualification of the offending Marian Avenue; it looked as if he was set to deliver a clear career-best; solid chance with that in mind.

RaceName: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Modest strike-rate and both wins were in 2024; has been chasing; 5lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: Three good runs over the winter (two of them from five runs in Bahrain) but he has struggled in two 7f runs back in Britain since April; handicapper slow to relent and this step up to 1m isn't sure to spark a revival.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: Dropped down the weights last summer and he has rediscovered his spark this time around, following third-placed efforts here and at Goodwood with a comfortable Salisbury success (all over 7f); probably stays this far but he has yet to be placed in his six 1m starts so it will be breaking new ground for him to win today.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: Won his first two starts for Karl Burke last year but he hasn't kicked on from his handicap debut second (1m, good to soft) last September (RPR 95); sold for 22,000gns in April; drops in class but not a solid option.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: Easy win at Lingfield (7f, good) on debut before pitched into Group 2 company for his second start; unsurprisingly came up short there but he was quickly back on track when defying a penalty at Southwell (7f) last October; bred to stay 1m and there should be more to come this year.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: C&D win last summer on his second start; similar form in defeat on his next two starts but he ended 2025 with a lesser effort and there was no revival on his reappearance at York (1m1f, good; first run since gelded; well backed) 19 days ago; dropped another 3lb and could benefit from the return to AW; still of interest.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
OwnerName: J Machale, S Tucker & S Jewell
Spotlight: Ran well over C&D last October and he was well backed when winning over 7f back here five days later; below his best at Chelmsford one day later; history doesn't suggest coming back from a break is the time to catch him.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: Ready 7f AW win in January; upped to 1m for the first time at Lingfield in March and produced career-best form, finishing off best behind a winner who was red-hot at the time; absent since but could have more to come at this trip.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)
Spotlight: C&D winner; ran creditably here three weeks ago but it was a lower grade and his finishing effort lacked oomph; likely vulnerable at this level.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cheap purchase; third in Naas barrier trial in May; slowly into stride and never a factor on racecourse debut at Fairyhouse (6f) last month; stable also runs Hip Hop Chrissy who holds stronger credentials.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
