Spotlight: Resold £36,000 yearling who didn't show much on sole start for Archie Watson at Nottingham (6f, good) four weeks ago; market will guide on Irish/stable debut.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sets a solid standard judged on his four maiden efforts to date, with marginally the best effort at Fairyhouse on penultimate (6f, good); not as good dropped to this trip at Down Royal (soft) 13 days ago but likely to be on the premises again.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Eclipse T'bred Partners/Gaynor Bloodstock LLC/F Gallagher
Spotlight: Second in Naas barrier trial in May; made a satisfactory debut five days later when third at Listowel (6.5f, good; the winner and runner-up ran at Royal Ascot subsequently); this opposition looks less demanding and she should have the speed for this trip.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not a bad effort on 5f debut at the Curragh when remote fourth, green before making modest late gains; ran to a similar level next time at Cork; perhaps may improve on better ground but will need to.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Hung right when not beaten far behind Bit Of A Buzz on 5f Cork debut; well beaten behind Hip Hop Chrissy when upped to 6.5f at Listowel; debut effort offers some hope.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Earthlight filly cost 20,000euros; wasn't far off Santo Parvenue when seventh of 10 on 5f Cork debut in May; likely improver; worth monitoring in the market.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; £2,500 yearling; half-sister to winners Jer Batt (5f inc AW; RPR 100) and Buddy Batt (5f inc 2yo/AW); dam 5f winner, half-sister to winners Another Batt (Turkish 6f 2yo Listed) and Iva Batt (5f/6f); market can guide.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sixth foal; 4,000euros yearling; sister to winners Marty Hopkirk (5f AW; RPR 91) and Commander Straker (5f 2yo; RPR 88), half-sister to Nuevo Slovo (5f 2yo); dam 7f/1m AW winner (81), half-sister to 6f Listed winner Katia; best watched.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Genealogy (1m 2yo; RPR 93), Pianoforte (5f; 86), Shahik (6f AW 2yo; 78) and Artifact (US 1m/8.5f); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (95) out of half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Finsceal Beo; same connections had a second-time-out 2yo winner on Monday.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: By Masar out of a French 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Group 2; RPR 111); that strongly suggests much longer trips than this but she made a bright debut, briefly threatening 1f out, when fourth of 12 in maiden here (6.5f, good to firm; 22-1) one month ago, so has to be considered.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Has run in two 6f novice events at Windsor, showing some improvement when rallying for fifth of ten on good to soft 24 days ago but that suggests she needs markedly better still; jockey bookings suggest that Royal Message is the stable first string.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 17-2, no match for a red-hot favourite (subsequent Listed winner) in five-runner novice at Bath (5f, good) 19 days ago but drew 5l clear of the rest, showing significant ability and promise.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: By a 6f/7f performer but there's stacks of stamina on the dam's side; 16-1, never dangerous from out the back when 8l fourth of eight behind an odds-on stablemate in maiden at Goodwood (6f, soft) three weeks ago, hampered at the start; probably capable of a lot better but whether she's sharp enough to eclipse all these rivals over 6f on good to firm is another question.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 38,000gns yearling by Australia; ninth foal; sister to 1m2f 2yo winner Anzac Day (RPR 99), half-sister to four winners including Merveillo (10.5f-1m4f including Listed; 99) and Ouezy (10.7f; 98); dam 1m3f winner, closely related to 1m 2yo Group 1/1m4f Grade 1 winner Ectot; pedigree suggests strongly that she is one for much further at a later stage.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Third foal; Havana Grey half-sister to 7f winner Back In Black (including 2yo; RPR 101); dam 7f 2yo winner (78), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Awesometank, out of 6f 2yo winner; 12-1 but upset in stalls when withdrawn from 6f Windsor novice (Bymiddaytomorrow 5-1) just over three weeks ago.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; Mehmas half-sister to 5f-7f winner Palmar Bay (including 2yo; RPR 100); dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/Listed; 106), sister to 7f 2yo and 1m3f Listed winner Dibayani; one to note from a leading yard.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 280,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Night Of Thunder half-sister to winners Attagirl (5f/6f 2yo including Listed; RPR 98), Skiathos (7f 2yo; 78) and Symbol Of Hope (5f/5.7f; 75); dam Italian 5f-6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 99), half-sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner; late foal but needs a close look.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 12,000gns foal, 62,000gns yearling; sixth foal; Starman half-sister to 1m-1m2f Flat (including 2yo; RPR 88) and 2m hurdle winner Into Battle; dam unraced half-sister to winners Johann Zoffany (Australian 1m6f Group 2) and Crown Choice (useful 6f/7f); powerful yard also runs Gravastar.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 25-1 and backward when 11l sixth of ten at Windsor (6f, good to soft) just over three weeks ago, struggling to stay in touch from halfway and Bymiddaytomorrow 8l ahead; should improve but can't be fancied just yet.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Lord Lloyd Webber & W S Farish
Spotlight: No Nay Never half-sister to 7f winner Champion Again Ole (RPR 86); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Group 2), from talented family; 16-1 when just over 7l fifth of eight in maiden at Leicester (6f, good to firm) five weeks ago, outpaced at halfway, but that was a hot race and she should improve; yard runs two and jockey bookings suggest that she is the stable first string.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 5,000gns foal; by Showcasing; third foal; dam 1m-1m4f winner (including AW Listed; RPR 107), half-sister to winners War Diary (9.7f/1m4f) and St Saviour (1m2f Flat/useful 2m hurdle), out of 1m-winning half-sister to five-time 1m Group 1 winner Attraction; trainer not known for 2yos.

RaceName: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 13-2 at Newmarket (6f, good) in May and came from rear to give the favourite a scare in closing stages; never showed when 20-1 for Group 3 at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) 13 days ago but the promising debut cannot yet be forgotten.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles but placed six times in maidens; soundly beaten in opening handicap run at Musselburgh and similar story back in maiden company at Tramore; tailed off in a Ballinrobe handicap in May (2m6f, good); blinkers go on now but others appeal more.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Well beaten on handicap debut here over 2m3f on soft to heavy ground but has finished runner-up in his two starts this season; beaten a short head at Punchestown (3m ,good to yielding) raised 3lb and was 2.25l behind Le Fanu at Listowel last time (2m5f, good to yielding); he's up 2lb more but his turn is coming.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Nice effort in third maiden hurdle start over 2m here in March when a 5l fifth of 12 (soft); okay sixth at Wexford on handicap bow (2m1f, good to yielding); more needed to play a starring role.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Sligo beginners' chase winner in October over 2m5f on yielding for Gordon Elliott; failed to build on that in three subsequent runs; makes stable debut/seasonal return back over hurdles now.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Won a Clonmel handicap in October (2m7f, yielding) off 73 and was in the lead when falling at Thurles next time; poor on return at Cork but won again back at Clonmel last month (2m7f, good), although dangerous-looking leader fell at two out; up 5lb taking account of rider's claim; in the mix but needs to prove she can do it away from Clonmel.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden from eight point starts and 11 under rules; tailed off over 2m4f here in May (soft) on stable debut; others more appealing.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 18th attempt at Listowel (2m5f, good to yielding) last time when 2.25l ahead of Illtakehimmyself; up 5lb but he should go well once again.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Made late headway in a couple of maidens and upped markedly in trip to 2m6f for his handicap debut at Downpatrick when a never nearer fourth (2m6f, yielding to soft); dropped 1lb and cheekpieces go on but better ground a question.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Won a 2m6f Tramore handicap hurdle in April last year (yielding) off 88; tailed off there back from a break in April (2m1f, good to yielding) but better effort on debut for current yard at Clonmel when sixth of 16 (2m4f, good), keeping on powerfully after not travelling early on; not dismissed in first-time tongue tie.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Didn't show anything in four maiden hurdle runs but much better on handicap bow at Tramore when a clear runner-up in first-time cheekpieces (2m6f, good, 33-1); up 3lb but of definite interest.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed promise in a bumper but not much in maiden hurdles; still held a chance when unseating at two out on handicap debut at Downpatrick (2m6f, good); poor at Sligo last time (2m5f, good).

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Point winner bagged a sole hurdles victory at Clonmel in 2024 (2m4f, good) off 2lb lower; tailed off over hurdles and pulled up over fences in two runs this year.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Ran her best race in her fourth handicap start at Punchestown in May (3m, good) when runner-up, well clear of the remainder; up 3lb but not at the same level when fifth at Wexford next time (3m, good to yielding); chance based on her Punchestown run.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Finished midfield in four of her five outings to date, including in her second handicap start here when upped to an extended 2m3f trip (soft to heavy), weakening before two out; up in distance again; has never raced on better than yielding ground.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Ordinary Flat form and tailed off at triple-figure prices in four maiden hurdles; the blinkers are new but hard to make a case for on handicap bow.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Winless from 11 starts (eight over hurdles); okay fifth of 12 in a moderate Wexford maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to yielding) in May; 19.5l behind at Listowel in May (2m5f, good to yielding).

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
OwnerName: Exors of the Late David Cotter
Spotlight: Yet to trouble the judge in seven runs over hurdles; went close over fences at Sligo in October (3m2f, good to yielding); tailed off at Thurles in October; pulled up back from a break in a Ballingarry point-to-point in May (evens fav'); others preferred.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Won AW handicaps at Kempton (7f) and Southwell (1m) last August and he's been in good form in the UAE this winter, including when runner-up at Abu Dhabi (1m, good) on his penultimate run; returns after 111 days off but he's strongly respected back on Polytrack.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Dual winner for Aidan O'Brien in his early days; his three wins this year have all been in claimers at Dundalk (6f-1m) but he travelled smoothly before winning with plenty in hand last time; with another new yard now but he remains well treated on old form and is not ruled out back in a handicap.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Won over C&D in April and he went close on Polytrack and turf in his next two starts; didn't really fire at Newmarket (1m, good) last time but he still looks on a workable mark and has claims if he can get back near best.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Ex-French gelding who made the breakthrough when landing the odds on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in May; flopped in his follow-up bid at Leicester but that was at 1m2f and the tacky ground may have been an issue; now drops back in trip and it wouldn't be a surprise if this unexposed 4yo resumes his progress.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Won at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) and Southwell (1m, AW) in his first two starts, but he's struggled in three of his four handicaps including when last of ten at Chester (10.3f) last month; has looked stretched in both attempts at that trip but he needs a major revival back on AW; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Dual course winner who scored twice at Wolverhampton (8.6f/7f) in March; hasn't threatened in his last four runs but he was respectable fifth over C&D on Monday; this is a quick turnaround but his last win was off this mark and he could be dangerous if he gets the breaks; cheekpieces reapplied.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Record of 4232 in novice/maiden events and was a good second on her handicap debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in October; looks interesting on that form but she's struggled in both subsequent runs (1m, good/soft) and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late G Kennington
Spotlight: All three wins have been at Kempton and latest was over C&D last June; now 1lb lower than for that success but he's been up and down since and was quiet on his reappearance here in May; comes with risks attached.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Triple turf winner who is 0-10 on AW but she returned after 202 days off with a respectable fourth over C&D in May; didn't get the best of runs in that race and has possibilities if she can build on that; regular hood is removed.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)
Spotlight: Eight-time AW winner who has finished runner-up over C&D in his last three starts including a close call on his penultimate run; should give it another good shot and he's respected.

RaceName: Indaver Ireland Handicap
Spotlight: Mostly campaigned (without success) in Listed company since winning off 77 at Chepstow (5f, gd-sft) last August; remains 10lb above that last success but a repeat of her 3l Cork fourth on penultimate would give her a squeak back in handicap company and she'll be suited by a strongly run race.

RaceName: Indaver Ireland Handicap
Spotlight: Speedy AW winner is 1-8 on turf; finished last season in fine form on AW but soundly beaten both starts this term, including on return to handicap company in the Epsom 3yo Dash last time out; eased 3lb and Keane an eyecatching booking; could make a bold bid from the front.

RaceName: Indaver Ireland Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to win as a 2yo but put that right when making all in a Navan maiden (5f, yielding) on his penultimate start in first-time cheekpieces; not beaten far on handicap debut back there in blinkers on latest (5f, good) and 1lb lower now; in the mix with cheekpieces back on.

RaceName: Indaver Ireland Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer a three-time winner at 6f on good to yielding and soft/heavy; mixed form since scoring at the Curragh on seasonal return in March off 1lb lower; C&D form figures of 343 so considered but may need softer ground than this.

RaceName: Indaver Ireland Handicap
Spotlight: Won a fillies' maiden at Navan (6f, good) last September; okay runs in defeat in handicaps this season including when fifth at 6.10 Fairyhouse Wednesday (joint-favoiurite); will be suited by a strongly run race at this distance and likely to get that here; chance

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Both runs over 6f on good ground in races won by Ballydoyle hotpots; second to the smart Confucius on debut and then fourth in the Group 3 Marble Hill won by subsequent Coventry winner Great Barrier Reef; the third has since gone close in another Group 3; faces promising opposition here but he sets the bar quite high.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 68,000euros yearling; second foal; half-brother to 6f-1m winner Fred On Fire (RPR 82); dam 1m4f winner (85); best to let the betting guide.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; first foal; dam placed 1m3f/1m4f AW (RPR 83), half-sister to useful 1m2f 2yo/1m4f winner Galactic Jack; one of two newcomers for the yard and Shakwaa appeals more.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; only 9-2 for his debut here over slightly further and went down narrowly having had every chance (on good to soft); presumably well thought of and expected to take a step forward second time out.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: £45,000 yearling; second foal out of an unraced half-sister to useful winners New Kingdom (7f/1m) and Cruyff Turn (7f/1m); interesting newcomer from a yard that's won five of the last ten runnings of this race.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 7.3f 2yo winner Torbay (RPR 54); dam 6f 2yo/7f AW winner (95), half-sister to 8.6f winner Dwight D; jumps-orientated yard is 1-22 with 2yos the last five seasons.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: B Dickson, P Cook & R Rexton
Spotlight: 60,000gns foal, 150,000gns yearling; brother to 5f 2yo Listed winner His Majesty (RPR 100) and half-brother to winners Coill Avon (6f; 93) and Spanish (1m; 90); dam a very smart 6f-1m winner; could be interesting.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: £100,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Aloha Star (5f/6f, including Group 2; RPR 103), Eye Smiling (6f; 73) and Zain Master (Italian 1m3f/12.5f Flat and 2m/2m1f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Battalion; newcomers from this yard are always of interest, especially here.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Oti Racing P Inglett S De Zoete &partner
Spotlight: 36,000gns foal, 80,000gns yearling; tenth foal; brother to winners Brayden Star (7.5f-1m4f, including Australian Group 2; RPR 109) and Prince Ali (7f-1m4f; 71), half-brother to four winners including Wentwood (1m-1m4f including Australian Group 3; 102); may need further and likely best watched unless market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 90,000gns foal, 210,000gns yearling; first foal of a US 1m/1m1f turf winner (RPR 100) and half-sister to Listed-placed 5f/5.5f winner Air Force Jet; looks a likely type on paper.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Dual-point winner bagged a first success under rules in May when keeping on well to win over C&D off 7lb lower (soft) and the runner-up won next time; has his chance of following up but this better ground and revised mark is a different test.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler in 2023, showing a liking for a test of stamina in testing conditions; now 7lb below his last winning mark but not much encouragement in his two Clonmel outings for his new stable.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won handicaps at Downpatrick (2m6f) and Down Royal (3m) last August; not as good at Listowel in September; pulled up on return at Clonmel (2m7f, good); needs to come back to her best.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Hunter chase winner at Tipperary last year has been hit or miss since; one of the hits came at Downpatrick last time when beaten 3.75l in third in a handicap chase (3m, good); reverts to hurdles now off 13lb lower mark but is 0-7 in this sphere without troubling judge.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 16-race maiden over hurdles; best form has been at Wexford; makes seasonal return after heavy defeats at Navan and Tipperary when last seen.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Off the mark at Clonmel in April (2m, soft) off 6lb lower; stamina test remains in doubt after moderate fifth at Tramore next time (2m5f, yielding); fell at the last when weakening on chase debut here last time (2m2f, good to yielding); capable of winning but beaten fav' last twice.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Now 0-17 but hit the frame a few times off higher marks for Tony Martin; in midfield when brought down at the second last at Downpatrick (2m6f, good top yielding) in a first-time tongue tie (discarded) on stable debut; may come on for that seasonal return, but needs to.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Doubled her hurdles tally at Kilbeggan in September (3m1f, good) off 5lb lower in first-time cheekpieces at 80-1; pulled up at Listowel when last seen later that month (2m6f, good to yielding); hard to trust, especially back from an absence.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Maiden's best efforts have come on good ground, including over this trip at Down Royal in April when third of 19 (3m, good) in this headgear; below that level but not disgraced in sixth at Wexford next time (3m, good to yielding); place claims.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Showed a bit in bumpers last year; never on terms when keeping on for sixth of 12 finishers at Kilbeggan in June (3m1f, good to yielding); down 1lb and he's well treated based on his early form.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-10 and was pulled up at Wexford on stable debut in May (2m1f, good to yielding); ran one of her best races at Clonmel over an inadequate extended 2m trip (good) when a 4l fifth, keeping on; this return to further should suit and she has her chance if she can find some consistency.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 10yo mare opened her account over hurdles at the 16th attempt at Clonmel in May (2m4f, good to yielding); distant second (winner DSQ) over 2m4f (soft) here off this 6lb higher mark last time; this ground suits better and she can continue to acquit herself well.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Runner-up in a 3m handicap at Wexford last summer (good); struggled after that, but her 9l fourth of 15 at Sligo (2m5f, good) last time was more promising; place chance if building on that.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Hasn't found much improvement over modest maiden hurdle form in handicaps in her last two runs; beaten 21l here when last seen in May (2m6f, soft); needs to find more.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-15 and yet to deliver on promise shown in 3yo hurdles in 2024; badly out of form over fences and hurdles in 2026; not easy to make a case for.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in point-to-points in May; once placed over hurdles but that was two years ago; no impact in a handicap chase at Roscommon last month.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Runner-up once over hurdles from nine starts (Wexford, 3m, good); sixth in opening two chase starts on better ground but was tailed off over an extended 3m on heavy at Punchestown when last seen in November; improvement required and may need this.

RaceName: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Winless from 33 starts under rules; recent efforts over fences here and hurdles at Kilbeggan suggest he's not going to change that today.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: R A Pegum And Paul Anthony Henry
Spotlight: Won last September on first two starts for yard; disappointing favourite at Newmarket (1m4f, good) on return but second of 13 over C&D (Bulletin fourth) five weeks ago was his best form yet; up 3lb but this race looks less competitive.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: A fairly useful dual winner (about 1m1f) in France for Andre Fabre in 2025, latterly on Polytrack; sold for 40,000gns in October and gelded; not seen since and needs to hit the ground running on debut for new yard.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual Kempton winner in 2023 and he's very well handicapped judged on form as recent as one year ago; Bahrain last winter did not go well for new connections, however, and although there was a whiff of his old form when seventh of 13 over C&D on his British return in May, he tailed off at Epsom ten days later; soft ground might excuse that but there are questions to answer.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Extraordinarily consistent sort who is yet to finish out of the frame in 16 starts; Master Vintner and two others proved too strong for him late on over C&D two starts back but that rival is 3lb worse off at today's weights, so they are very closely matched.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Well treated judged on peak form (AW in spring 2025) but it's a serious concern that his latest start (five weeks ago; made most) so seriously damaged his record of going well when back from a break; that was the first time he's been tongue tied (as he is today) and also his first race at Kempton.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Two wins from six starts since joining Hayley Burton, both over 1m4f on Lingfield Polytrack; dictated the pace on second occasion and he's not been in quite such good form on either start since, albeit with 2m looking a stretch on latest; probably on the premises.

RaceName: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Winner of a bumper and three novice hurdle races, most recently a Grade 2 here in February 2025; chasing didn't work out but he mostly ran with credit in defeat back over hurdles and three Flat races have been thrown in (1m3f here latest) in which he's shown some ability; it's not clear what his ideal Flat trip is but he's now qualified for a Flat handicap mark and it's a highly attractive one compared to his hurdles figure.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Much-improved effort on debut for this stable at Navan (1m5f, good) 25 days ago when keeping on for a 1.25l fourth of 16; should go well again off 1lb higher if in the same mood.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose veteran; winner of nine races across both codes; bolted up at Sligo in May (1m6f, yld-sft) off 6lb lower; struggled the last twice however, and previous runs here have not been his best.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Gained reward for a string of solid efforts when always well positioned and winning at Navan (1m5f, good) 25 days ago; 6lb higher now but rider's 2lb claim partly negates that; consistent sort is a big player again.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden has shown glimmers, including when a 5.25l fifth at Navan on latest (1m5f, good); weakened late having led that day so still has stamina to prove in first-time tongue-tie.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally
Spotlight: Just 1-21 on the Flat but not beaten far the last twice; closely matched with Kevin The Great on Navan running earlier this month; didn't quite see out 2m2f at Down Royal latest; goes well here and big chance back down in trip off last winning mark for local yard.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Best run yet for this yard on seasonal return at Leopardstown where a 2.5l fourth of 19 (1m5f, good); same mark here and should strip fitter now so enters calculations.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins on the Flat but hasn't won on turf since June 2024; 4lb below that last success but recent form hasn't indicated that a return to winning ways is imminent; others preferred.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden ran well when a 3.75l fourth of 16 at Roscommon in May for this rider but hasn't backed that up; beaten 15l when down the field at Fairyhouse (1m4f, good) on latest.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden hasn't run that badly in two of her three starts in handicaps; kept on well enough over 1m4f at Limerick on latest; dam closely related to 2m winner so may appreciate this longer trip but needs more to get her head in front.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo didn't show a whole lot in maidens and similar story in first two handicaps; always behind when beating just one rival home at Down Royal latest (2m, good); hard to fancy for now.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: A couple of her runs in 2024 suggested she could win a race but missed almost all of 2025 and heavy defeat on return from long layoff at Down Royal last month; cheekpieces go on and she's on a dangerous mark on her best form so check the market.

RaceName: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't won since 2023 and was struggling for form but took a step in the right direction when not far behind a couple of these at Navan last time; best form on easy ground, however, so could do with some rain.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: He's gone 0-12 in handicaps but is edging down the weights and ran well to finish fourth of ten at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last time (form has been franked); this season's runs have been in Class 3s so this is a drop in grade.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Won two AW races for Andrew Balding whom he left for 60,000gns; he's not always looked entirely straightforward but he was less than 2l away last time at Newmarket (7f, good) after being in rear at one point; Tom Marquand takes over now; respected.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: 7f AW novice winner at two; 0-16 in handicaps but yet another sound effort in defeat last time at Newmarket (7f, good); handicapped about right but one of these can come his way.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Last year's wins were at about 1m and hasn't raced over 7f since his debut; quiet this season after a gelding operation and others appeal more; cheekpieces, in which he has form figures of 100, now return.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
OwnerName: K Mills, J Brown, S Barley
Spotlight: 7f novice winner for Ed Walker; made a successful stable debut off 4lb lower in March (7f, good to soft) but hasn't kicked on from there.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
OwnerName: Total Asbestos Solutions Ltd & A Frost
Spotlight: Four wins divided between turf and the AW; 7lb above his highest winning mark and appeared to have no excuses when fifth of 14 at Wetherby four weeks ago (7f, good to firm).

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice at around 1m two years ago; close third over 6f at Newmarket on final run of last season and signs of edging back into form when beaten 4l in a Class 2 at York 20 days ago (6f); returning to further off a handy mark, she's one to consider.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Novice winner who was close up in a C&D handicap last August off 1lb higher; major claims if able to repeat that form, which hasn't been the case in three subsequent races.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Back from a break to claim career win number six on the Kempton AW (7f) in March but then disappointed at Yarmouth (off this 3lb higher mark); consistent in the main since sent into handicaps and breaks tend to do him good.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: On a hat-trick after improved efforts to win over 1m on good ground at Thirsk and Goodwood (both under Joe Leavy); she was all out last time in a race that panned out favourably but a 4lb rise was fair and this is only her eighth race, albeit her first not over a mile.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs M T Bevan & A Bevan
Spotlight: It wasn't a strong 7f novice that he won on good ground at Lingfield but he came clear at the finish and brings potential into handicaps; one to be interested in.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Exposed four-time winner; has run a number of good races this season and Doncaster last time was one of those, as he did well to finish so close after missing the break; not without hope.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Her second in a 7f novice at Newmarket last October is very much a standout; 11-1 and beat only two home in a 7f handicap there in May.

RaceName: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Her two 7f wins (good/soft) came at a cost mark-wise and she could finish only sixth at Sandown; this is her first time at this loftier level and she needs a career best by some margin.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Weakened tamely at Punchestown on bumper debut but much better in an added tongue tie (retained) at Ballinrobe when a clear runner-up (Time For Talking behind in third); Derek O'Connor is back aboard and big run expected.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Clear second behind easy Willie Mullins winner on debut at Roscommon (yielding to soft); that run puts him on the shortlist with normal improvement.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
OwnerName: A Walk In The Park Syndicate
Spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding; dam maiden in points and 2m-2m4f hurdles, closely related to winners Offaly (bumper/2m4f hurdle/2m5f chase) and Blencathra Bay (bumper); top rider aboard and in good hands so worth a market check.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Fine Flat pedigree; sold by Godolphin unraced for 40,000gns last autumn; 11-1 for bumper debut at Killarney (2m1f, good yielding) but raced wide and was well beaten; much more required.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: 16,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; half-brother to bumper/2m7f hurdle winner Chance The Robin; unraced dam closely related to high-class bumper and 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Snoopy Loopy, half-sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Sam Adams; others make more appeal.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Did well on debut to take third at Ballinrobe, particularly as his rider lost his balance and irons after a few furlongs before regaining them; 3.5l behind Bacharach on that occasion and 5lb worse off now so work to do.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Parish Star, half-brother to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Eastern Voice (RPR 90); dam maiden (61) sister to 6f 2yo/2m hurdle winner Freedom Square, half-sister to useful 1m4f Flat/2m hurdle winner Via Galilei; best watched.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Half-sister to winners Henschke (2m5f/3m1f chase), Reserve Judgement (2m7f/3m1f hurdle) and Nanny Betty (point); dam unraced half-sister to 2m5f-3m hurdle/chase winner Letbeso, family of high-class staying chasers Grey Abbey and The Grey Monk; trainer 0-33 in bumpers last five seasons.

RaceName: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)
Spotlight: Walk In The Park filly the first foal out of top-class racemare Benie Des Dieux; despite her pedigree, she was relatively unfancied on her debut at Listowel and finished a distant third; needs to show a good bit more.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won a Newcastle maiden (6f, Tapeta) for George Boughey last October, but he's struggled in four runs for new yard this year; continues to drop down the weights but he needs a major revival back on AW; blinkers removed.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Made a winning debut in a newcomers' race at Deauville (6.5f, AW) last October, but well held in three starts on turf since; still unexposed but he was withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Newbury last week and others are preferred; equipment removed.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Maiden winner (7.5f, good) for Jessica Harrington last August but he's finished down the field in three subsequent runs including on handicap/stable debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) two weeks ago; still lightly raced but he needs to raise his game.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won a 5f maiden at Catterick last summer but he's been expensive to follow in handicaps this year; now 9lb lower than for his opening mark but he's been well held in last three runs and needs blinkers to make a difference.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
OwnerName: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc
Spotlight: Has a patchy profile but she improved her record to 2-9 when scoring with cheekpieces added at Goodwood (6f, soft) last month; 5lb higher from wide draw here, but that was a personal best last time and her last visit to Kempton resulted in a good second over C&D; respected.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Promising fourth on Lingfield debut in December before a last-gasp win over 6f at Chelmsford (7-2) the following month; well held in final run during the winter but he raced wide in that 7f novice; returns after 162 days off but he's still unexposed and needs a close back in trip on handicap debut.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Showed some promise in her qualifying runs including when fourth of 11 at Chester (7f, good) in May; has shaped as though this drop back in trip could suit and she needs watching in market on handicap debut.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding; dead-heated in a Chelmsford maiden (6f, AW) on second run for current yard but he's been well held in two handicaps since; has bit to prove again and overall record record is now 1-7.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Six-race maiden for two different yards but he ran into some trouble when a fair third in Ffos Las handicap (5f, good to soft) last month; dropped another 2lb and he could be dangerous on this step back up in trip.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won a Yarmouth maiden (6f, good to firm) for William Haggas last August; has mixed record since but she got back near her best when third at Newcastle (6f, AW) in May; 1lb lower after a short break and she's a player if she can back that up.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Encouraging return when third of ten in a C&D maiden in May and although only third of four at Windsor (6f, good to firm) three weeks later, the winner and fourth have won since; opening mark is no gift but it's still early days and he should have more to offer.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Suited by the drop to 6f when scoring on handicap debut at Windsor (good to firm) in May; only 2lb higher here but he lost his action and was pulled up at Windsor two weeks ago; needs to bounce back after that and he still has a bit to prove on AW.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
Spotlight: Useful jumper made a bright start on the Flat, winning this race at skinny odds last July then following up in another Q.R. race over 1m6f at Listowel; soundly beaten in Curragh handicap on seasonal return latest but this is easier and he's best treated by race conditions; obvious chance.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
OwnerName: Mrs Sheila O'Ryan & John F Doyle & Grant Leon
Spotlight: Dual-purpose sort has been running well on the Flat; two solid efforts in Curragh handicaps to sign off last season; okay effort in Q.R. race at Listowel in May and advertised wellbeing with good second in Curragh charity race last week; player.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
OwnerName: Michael J Mulligan & Patrick G Walsh
Spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles at Ballinrobe (2m, good) on debut for this yard in late May off a mark of 105; now makes Flat debut and probably up against it on these terms.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
Spotlight: Talented over jumps; rated 137 over hurdles and a five-time winner in that sphere; has struggled in chases on last few starts and has been off since March but returns with local yard among the winners and he bolted up in a bumper here back in 2023; check the market.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
Spotlight: A winner of six races (four over hurdles and two on the level); however, a Flat rating of just 40 leaves him with heaps to find on these unfavourable terms.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
Spotlight: Rated just 34 on the Flat and no win of any description since 2020; up against it.

RaceName: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race
OwnerName: Thomas Cox/Nap Racing Syndicate/James M Nolan
Spotlight: Two-time Flat winner when trained in France; has run a couple of decent races for this yard and one of his better efforts came when beaten just 2.5l by Lark In The Mornin when third in this last year; 10lb better off now and should strip fitter for his okay comeback run at Navan; chance.

RaceName: Local IQ Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive; 2-2 since fitted with cheekpieces, supplementing a convincing 7f Lingfield win with another over 1m at Chepstow (good to firm, 4-6 favourite); this is a sterner test from 10lb higher than last time but, with fast ground to suit, it'll be no surprise if he's up to the challenge.

RaceName: Local IQ Handicap
Spotlight: Made all at Salisbury (7f novice, soft; 11-1) three weeks ago; it isn't easy to know exactly what to make of that - runner-up was a 400-1 shot - but, closely related to the 1m2f winner Capla Lazarus, she's open to improvement on handicap debut over 1m.

RaceName: Local IQ Handicap
Spotlight: With Joseph Parr when an eyecatching fourth at Leicester (7f; 150-1) last September; produced only a fair opening effort for this trainer, but stepped up to score at Lingfield (7.5f, soft; 2-1 favourite) a fortnight later; interesting to see what he makes of handicapping now with Tom Marquand booked.

RaceName: Local IQ Handicap
Spotlight: Still seeking breakthrough (0-6), going closest when third at Nottingham (8.5f, good; 4-1 favourite) in April; soft ground given as the reason for a less-convincing effort at Lingfield (1m1f; fourth of seven, 5-2 favourite) latest.

RaceName: Local IQ Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't offer much on his first two starts for Donnacha O'Brien, and only marginally better when a staying-on fourth at Dundalk (1m maiden; 6-1, beaten over 12l) in February; best watched on first start for Tony Carroll; handicap debut.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Three-time course winner over 7f for his previous stable but was third over this trip at Windsor in September; well held in both starts since returning from a break and first-time blinkers now replace cheekpieces; dropped another 3lb.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Missed 2025 but ran well when second of seven over this C&D in March on his first start for 702 days; struggled on his next two starts but returned to form when going close at Ffos Las (5f) nine days ago; this dual C&D will be suited by the return to this trip and has strong claims.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner, most recently at Lingfield (6f) in April off 2lb lower; well held at Yarmouth on his last two starts but has an excuse three weeks ago (reared leaving the stalls); returning to the AW could suit.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Four-time AW winner, most recently at Lingfield (6f) in January off the same mark as today's; left the impression he's still in form on soft ground at Salisbury 23 days ago and has place claims back on the AW.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: C&D winner in January off a 1lb higher mark than today's and he's posted some good efforts since, including when fifth of 18 in a 0-80 at Ascot in May; beaten about 3l when sixth at Goodwood (7f, soft) 25 days ago and now drops in grade.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 15-race maiden but he did finish runner-up on three occasions last year (twice on the AW); however, he's been down the field in three starts this year and drops back in trip with a bit to prove; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 3-35, with the latest success coming at Lingfield (7f, AW) in December off a mark 8lb higher than today's; never featured over this trip at Wolverhampton 31 days ago and better is needed.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner (made all both times), including when beating Travel Agent in April; below his best on turf next time but he's been placed on the AW in both starts since; contender.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won a Leicester novice (6f, good to firm) last July and he was third of nine in a handicap at Newcastle (6f, AW) in January; down the field on his final start for Andrew Balding and now makes his stable debut (bought for 10,000gns) after 153 days off; watch the betting.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: C&D winner early last year when trained by Phil McEntee and his other success came on the AW at Southwell in October; reported to have lost both shoes when fifth at Newbury (6f, good to firm) nine days ago and may come on for that first start since January; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner who went close at Lingfield (5f) in March and ran well there over 6f in April, finishing third of five (beaten a length); never got involved there 28 days ago but he could go well returned to 6f.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: All three wins came on turf when trained by Hugo Palmer, the most recent at Chester in September; 0-12 on the AW but he did post a solid effort when a 1l fourth of seven at Lingfield (5f, AW) 28 days ago; steps back up in trip.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Three good turf runs to his name this summer, all for today's apprentice Chloe Lyons and including C&D; no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well once again.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer; off the mark for the year with a narrow win at Bath (5f, good) in May; held her form since and her latest Sandown third came in a higher grade; Ashley Lewis knows her well and she's a leading contender.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: On the up for new stable this year, fourth at Windsor on his return in May before getting off the mark in a Class 6 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 17 days ago; still looked to be learning his job on that occasion and a 5lb rise is unlikely to prove beyond him; apprentice rider Harry Vigors making a big name for himself; lots to like.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo; she has found the going tougher this time around but the handicapper is relenting and this should be more suitable; others still look more appealing; stable runs two.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: On the up on AW in the spring, winning three 5f handicaps at Southwell; signs that the handicapper is catching up more recently and others appeal more.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Shaped well on his stable debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) in February but hamstrung by slow starts on his next three starts; further blotted his copybook when refusing to race at Windsor in May; well backed back at Windsor (5f, good to firm) ten days ago and again not foot perfect leaving the stalls but he overcame some adversity (stumbled early and had to be switched in the straight) to win going away; little doubt he has the ability to defy his penalty but there are risks attached.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: She made a bright start to her career last summer, winning a Chelmsford maiden and a Salisbury novice (both 6f); struggling in 2026 though and opposable despite her reduced mark.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Twice a winner over C&D, including the corresponding event 12 months ago when rated 11lb higher; back to form when second at Windsor ten days ago, failing to hold the late thrust of Just Jump; his supporters should get a run for their money but he could just prove vulnerable late on.

RaceName: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to win in nine runs for Dylan Cunha; may come on for her recent stable debut (after 102 days off) but she needs to.
